channel.title: IBD LitMonitor - Quality of Life & PROs
channel.description: IBD literature monitoring: Quality of Life & PROs

item.title: Evaluating the quality and reliability of inflammatory bowel disease-related short videos on BiliBili and TikTok: A cross-sectional study.
item.description: Health informatics journal - BackgroundInflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD) is a chronic condition with increasing global prevalence. Short-video platforms like TikTok and Bilibili are popular health information sources, but their...

item.title: Extraintestinal manifestations have a negative impact on Quality of Life of patients with Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Digestion - Inflammatory Bowel Diseases (IBD) can produce extraintestinal manifestations (EIMs) in up to 50% of patients with IBD and may have worse quality of life than the general population. In general, IBD...

item.title: TAAR Immunopharmacology.
item.description: Handbook of experimental pharmacology - Trace amine-associated receptors (TAARs) were originally identified as G protein-coupled receptors involved in monoaminergic signaling within the central nervous system. However, accumulating evide...

item.title: The shifting burden of early-onset inflammatory bowel disease: a global analysis.
item.description: Pediatric research - Early-onset inflammatory bowel disease (EO-IBD), affecting children under age 10 years, has an emerged as a distinct global pediatric health concern with increasing clinical and epidemiological fea...

item.title: Inflammatory Bowel Disease and Its Unique Impact on Body Image: A Qualitative Exploration.
item.description: Journal of clinical psychology - There is increasing interest in the relationship between inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and body image. Quantitative research demonstrates a high level of body image dissatisfaction among individ...

item.title: Hierarchical correlates of sexual dysfunction in inflammatory bowel disease: Psychological burden outweighs disease activity and sarcopenia.
item.description: Journal of psychosomatic research - Sexual dysfunction (SD) is a common but underrecognized complication of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), arising from a complex interplay of biological, psychological, and social factors. Their re...

item.title: Effect of physician prescribed information to the inflammatory bowel disease patients on quality of life and disease relapse: a randomised control trial.
item.description: Journal of the Medical Library Association : JMLA - RCT - Inflammatory Bowel Diseases (IBD), including Ulcerative Colitis and Crohn&s Disease, are chronic and recurrent conditions that severely impact patients' quality of life and increase the risk of com...

item.title: Nanomedicines for modulating the gut-brain axis.
item.description: Nanomedicine (London, England) - The dysregulation of the gut-brain axis affects cerebral function, contributing to the occurrence of neuropsychiatric symptoms and the worsening of neurodegenerative disorders. The main and direct ...

item.title: Application of thermo-responsive polymers as an enema formulation for the treatment of ulcerative colitis.
item.description: International journal of pharmaceutics - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory bowel disease characterized by abdominal pain and diarrhea. Although 5-aminosalicylic acid (5-ASA) enemas are widely used, conventional formulation...

item.title: The PANACEA Project: a multinational model for digital health literacy in inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Crohn's & colitis 360 - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) represents an increasing public health challenge in Latin America, where structural inequities and limited access to standardized education contribute to gaps in he...

item.title: Mitochondrial Dysfunction in Ulcerative Colitis: Pathogenic Mechanisms and Novel Therapeutics.
item.description: Journal of biochemical and molecular toxicology - Review - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory bowel disease driven by a complex interplay of mitochondrial dysfunction. These defects impair energy production, increases oxidative stress, and d...

item.title: Empowering healthier lifestyles: patterns of digital tool use, self-efficacy, and affective balance in IBD-patients and a community sample.
item.description: Frontiers in digital health - When reaching health-related personal strivings, consistent self-management is crucial, which can be supported by digital technology use and online resources. Prior research highlights the importan...
